body.title: ContainerShim: pin network isolation model before implementation (bridge vs none + unix socket)
body.result: Pinned --network=none + Unix socket; ValueError on docker.sock mount; TheoryOfOperation §2.3 added
body.description:
MEDIUM gap found in T-murderbot-security-review.

T-container-shim spec says 'loopback socket to the shim only, no host network' but does not pin the Docker network model. The two plausible interpretations have different security properties:

- '--network=none' + Unix domain socket: container has no TCP stack, only the socket. Strong isolation. The shim and container must share a socket path via a bind mount.
- Bridge network + port mapping: container gets a network namespace with a gateway IP. Containers on a bridge CAN reach the host via the gateway address. If host services (Postgres, IMAP) bind to 0.0.0.0 they are reachable from bridged containers -- bypassing the shim.

The current spec is ambiguous. Before T-container-shim implementation begins, this must be pinned. Recommended: --network=none + Unix domain socket for the shim channel. Also: explicitly prohibit /var/run/docker.sock in allowed_mounts in ContainerShim.start() regardless of profile, to prevent escape via Docker API.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/skeleton/container_shim.py (not yet created -- this ticket gates implementation), docs/TheoryOfOperation.md (section 2.3 Security Tiers)
**Design rules:** BaseDevice/BaseShim contract
**Scope boundary:** IN -- pin network model to --network=none + unix socket; explicit docker socket deny; update TheoryOfOperation 2.3; OUT -- VM-level isolation, container image registry
**Completion criteria:** ContainerShim.start() uses --network=none; communicates via Unix socket; raises ValueError if allowed_mounts contains docker.sock; TheoryOfOperation 2.3 states the network model and the why
